name: rf/lossy_waveguide_attenuation
description: Lossy-wall TE10 guide (surface impedance): FDTD conductor attenuation alpha from the two-line |S21| ratio vs analytic skin-effect alpha_c.
reference: analytic-TE10 conductor attenuation (Pozar 3.96; R_s=sqrt(w mu0/2 sigma))
target: FDTD alpha from two-line |S21| within 5% of analytic TE10 alpha_c, GATED on cond(A) + passivity at both lengths
tolerance_basis: Two-stage (terminated two-port precedent): (1) wave-level precondition -- the B=S*A extraction is well conditioned (cond(A) <= 10) and the solved S passive (max singular value <= 1.05) at both guide lengths and every frequency; (2) if met, the two-line conductor attenuation alpha is within the pre-registered 5% of the analytic TE10 alpha_c. The SIBC runtime evaluates the wall surface resistance R_s at the source frequency (narrowband good-conductor order-0 model), so the analytic R_s and the runtime surface resistance are the SAME quantity -- the bench tests that the surface-impedance wall reproduces the skin-effect loss, not a fitted constant.
falsification: EXECUTED: the attenuation must come from the WALL LOSS, not the fixture. A PEC-wall guide of identical geometry is lossless -- its two-line |S21| ratio is ~1 and the extracted alpha collapses to 0.0002 Np/m (vs the good-conductor 1.8387 Np/m at 2.4 GHz). The two-line alpha also scales as 1/delta_L, so a 10%-wrong length shifts it 10% (>> the 5% gate).

metrics.note: TE10 cutoff; band 2.0-2.8 GHz all propagating; sigma=25 S/m walls

metrics.quantity: alpha from two-line |S21| (median rel error over band)
metrics.note: vs analytic TE10 alpha_c; cond(A)=1.02, max sv=0.363, max rel 0.37%

supporting.quantity: alpha (external reference solver, TE10 mode-source decay)
supporting.note: ln(|amp_in|/|amp_out|)/L_ref of the reference forward TE10 vs analytic alpha_c; median 93.12%, max 94.54% over 3 frequencies (normalization-independent, one cloud run). DIVERGES: the external lossy-metal surface-impedance export under-applies the wall loss at this coarse export grid (the phase-based waveguide beta cross-check on the SAME adapter path agrees to ~1%), so the discrepancy is a documented external lossy-metal export fidelity gap, NOT the FDTD bench.

notes: SIBC skin-effect wave-level bench: four PEC waveguide walls replaced by a single shared good-conductor surface-impedance boundary. The terminated two-port S is well conditioned (cond(A) 1.02) and passive (max singular value 0.363); the two-line conductor attenuation agrees with the analytic TE10 alpha_c to 0.05% (median over 3 in-band frequencies, max 0.37%), inside the pre-registered 5% gate. Per-frequency measured/analytic alpha, |S21| at both lengths, and the conditioning/passivity diagnostics are in the artifact conservation block.

Reference policy: the analytic TE10 conductor attenuation is the binding first-line reference. The external-reference cross-check is recorded separately by benchmark.rf_tidy3d_references (the lossy-metal wall exports through the adapter's dedicated surface path); its runnable/generation status is surfaced in the Tidy3D ref column and the RF/antenna external reference section.

External-reference-solver cross-check (TE10 ModeSource lossy guide, one cloud run, task recorded in the reference marker): the reference forward-mode attenuation is 93% below the analytic alpha_c (|amp_in|/|amp_out| ~ 1.08 vs the analytic ~3x decay over the 0.6 m baseline). The external lossy-metal RF surface-impedance export under-applies the wall loss at this coarse export grid, while the phase-based waveguide beta cross-check on the SAME adapter path agrees to ~1% -- so the gap is specific to the lossy-metal surface-impedance export fidelity, a documented adapter limitation, NOT the FDTD bench (which matches the analytic alpha_c to 0.05%). The analytic alpha_c remains the binding reference.
